1: Now it came to pass after the death of Saul, when David was returned from the slaughter of the Amalekites, and David had abode two days in Ziklag;
2: it came even to pass on the third day, that, behold, a man came out of the camp from Saul with his clothes rent, and earth upon his head: and so so it was, when he came to David, that he fell to the earth, and did obeisance.
3: And David said unto him, From whence comest thou? And he said unto him, Out of the camp of Israel am I escaped.
4: And David said unto him, How went the matter? I pray thee, tell me. And he answered, That the people are fled from the battle, and many of the people also are fallen and dead; and Saul and Jonathan his son are dead also.
5: And David said unto the young man that told him, How knowest thou that Saul and Jonathan his son be dead?
6: And the young man that told him said, As I happened by chance upon mount Gilboa, behold, Saul leaned upon his spear; and, lo, the chariots and horsemen followed hard after him.
7: And when he looked behind him, he saw me, and called unto me. And I answered, Here am am I.
8: And he said unto me, Who art art thou? And I answered him, I am am an Amalekite.
9: He said unto me again, Stand, I pray thee, upon me, and slay me: for anguish is come upon me, because my life is is yet whole in me.
10: So I stood upon him, and slew him, because I was sure that he could not live after that he was fallen: and I took the crown that was was upon his head, and the bracelet that was was on his arm, and have brought them hither unto my lord.
11: Then David took hold on his clothes, and rent them; and likewise all the men that were were with him:
12: and they mourned, and wept, and fasted until even, for Saul, and for Jonathan his son, and for the people of the LORD LORD , and for the house of Israel; because they were fallen by the sword.
13: And David said unto the young man that told him, Whence art art thou? And he answered, I am am the son of a stranger, an Amalekite.
14: And David said unto him, How wast thou not afraid to stretch forth thine hand to destroy the LORD LORD 's anointed?
15: And David called one of the young men, and said, Go near, and and fall upon him. And he smote him that he died.
16: And David said unto him, Thy blood be be upon thy head; for thy mouth hath testified against thee, saying, I have slain the LORD LORD 's anointed.
17: And David lamented with this lamentation over Saul and over Jonathan his son:
18: (Also he bade them teach the children of Judah the use of the use of the bow: behold, it is it is written in the book of Jasher.)
20: Lest the daughters of the uncircumcised triumph.
21: The shield of Saul, as though he had as though he had not been been anointed with oil.
22: And the sword of Saul returned not empty.
24: Who put on ornaments of gold upon your apparel.
25: O Jonathan, thou wast thou wast slain in thine high places.
27: And the weapons of war perished!
